dialogues.precomment:
You are running.#

Efeliah's scream still echoes off the canyon walls around you,# but your legs are already moving,#
propelled by something deeper than thought.#
Instinct.#
The raw, animal certainty that something is very, very wrong.#

Smiurl is ahead of you, scrambling over rocks with a nimbleness impossible for a human.
Corolla is right behind you,# her breathing sharp and controlled.
Theo brings up the rear, his heavy footsteps like muffled thunder.#

Math is holding Efeliah's arm, half-guiding, half-pulling her as she runs with her eyes wide open,#
staring at something none of you can see.##

Her voice slides into your mind:
dialogues.line: Minds.# Hundreds of them.# Coming towards us through the Mazerock.
What kind of minds? Hordes? Oxies?
No.## Something... wrong.# They are there but... they are not.# Like empty rooms with the lights still on.
dialogues.comment: Her telepathic voice trembles.# You have never felt that in her before.

dialogues.line: She is right. I can sense them too now. A single will, wearing hundreds of bodies!
dialogues.comment: Now, that doesn't sound promising at all.

dialogues.precomment:
You keep running, your sword banging against the narrow rock walls, sparks flying off the stone.#
The canyons twist and turn, one passage bleeding into another.#
You have no idea where you are going.# Nobody does.
dialogues.line: How far?!
Close.# Very close.# We need to hide.# Now!!

nodes.text:
Theo reacts before anyone else.# He grabs Smiurl under one arm like a sack of flour and surges forward,#
scanning the rock walls with the focus of a man who has done this before.##

Then you hear it.##

At first you think it is your own heartbeat,# amplified by the canyon acoustics.#
But the rhythm is wrong.# Too regular.# Too mechanical.##

Footsteps.##

Hundreds of footsteps,# striking stone in $Wperfect unison$C.##

No voices.# No clanking of armour.# No shuffling.#
Just the metronomic, relentless percussion of feet hitting rock at exactly the same instant.##

$c<<What the hell makes that sound?>>#

$CTheo veers sharply left into a crevice you would have missed entirely,#
a jagged tear in the canyon wall barely wide enough for a person.#
He shoves Smiurl in first, then Math and Efeliah.#
Corolla pushes you in ahead of her and squeezes in last, pressing flat against you.#

Theo stands at the entrance, his massive frame filling the gap,#
one hand braced flat against each wall.##

The footsteps grow louder.##

Louder.###

The first figures pass the mouth of your crevice.##

You press yourself against the cold stone, holding your breath,#
and through the narrow gap between Theo's shoulder and the rock wall, you see them.##

Soldiers.##

Men and women, marching in perfect lockstep.#
Their faces are...## blank.##

Not sleeping.# Not unconscious.# The eyes are open,#
staring straight ahead with a terrible, vacant fixity.#
Every face wears the same expression:# none.## No expression at all.##

Across each forehead, glinting dully in the dim canyon light,#
a thin $Wmetal headband$C.##

You count.# Ten.# Twenty.# Fifty.# They keep coming.##

They carry weapons.# Swords, spears, crude pikes.#
Held at exactly the same angle by every hand.#
Arms that do not sway.# Legs that do not falter.#
Bodies that move with the horrifying precision of puppets on the same string.##

And the $Wsilence$C.###

No one speaks.# No one coughs, or clears their throat, or shifts a weapon from one hand to the other.#
No boots scuff.# No armour rattles.# There is no breathing you can hear,#
just the footsteps,## hundreds upon hundreds of them,## landing as one.##

You see a boy among them.# He cannot be older than fifteen.##
Same headband.# Same dead eyes.# Same metronomic stride.#
His hands are too small for the pike he carries.##

$c<<They are not soldiers.# They are not anything anymore.>>#

$CYou feel Corolla's hand close around your forearm.#
Her grip is iron.# She squeezes so hard the bruise will last for days,#
but neither of you acknowledges it.##

Behind you, Efeliah makes a sound.#
A thin, keening whimper, like a dog that has been struck.#
Math is holding her,# one hand pressed gently over her mouth,# his own face twisted with concentration.#

$c<<The psychic void.# She can feel all of them and there is nothing inside.>>#

$CSmiurl is pressed into the deepest corner of the crevice.#
You glance back at him and your stomach drops.##

His skin is $Wdark$C.#
Completely dark.# Not a single flicker of bioluminescence.#
You have never seen that before.##
Not even when the oxengutters attacked.# Not even in the canyon with the Chief.##

The procession continues.##

And continues.##

And continues.###

You lose track of time.# Your legs are cramping.# Corolla's hand has gone numb on your arm.#
Theo has not moved a single muscle.##

Finally,# the last row passes.#
The footsteps recede into the distance,# growing fainter,#
fainter,##
until they are swallowed by the stone.###

Silence.##

Real silence, this time.#
The kind with wind in it, and the distant call of a bird.##

Nobody moves for a long time.

Theo steps back from the crevice entrance slowly, releasing a long, controlled breath.#
One by one, you emerge from the crack in the rock, legs stiff, hearts pounding.

Efeliah sinks to the ground the moment she is free, Math kneeling beside her.#
She is trembling.# Her hands are shaking so badly she cannot hold them still.

dialogues.line: What were they.

dialogues.precomment: Math takes a moment before answering. He looks older than you have ever seen him.
dialogues.line:
People.# Or what is left of them.#
The headbands are psychic conduits.# They erase the individual mind and replace it with a signal.#
A single controlling will.# Every one of those people is being piloted. Like a machine wearing skin.
I could feel where their minds used to be.# Like... scars. Outlines of people who were there once.
They are still in there, somewhere. Buried. Screaming.# And nobody can hear them.

dialogues.precomment:
Corolla is standing apart, staring in the direction the procession disappeared.#
When she turns back, her eyes are hard.
dialogues.line:
They have been turned into tools.# Walking, breathing tools.
I have seen what Hordes do to people.# This is worse.

Yes.# The legends spoke of Ayonn's power, but I never imagined... this.
A single mind, controlling hundreds. Perhaps thousands.
Whatever is generating that signal — it is ancient. Powerful. And it is coming from the city.
Then we go to the city.
We WHAT?!# You just saw what those... those THINGS were!# We will end up like them!
dialogues.comment:
Smiurl's bioluminescence flickers between orange and deep red — colours you have never seen on him before.#

Everyone looks at you.

nodes.text: Smiurl is looking at you with eyes full of fear.# Everyone is waiting for you to speak.

choices.desc: Put your hand on his shoulder. "We'll get through this. Together."
Be honest with him. "I won't lie to you. This is worse than anything we've faced."

nodes.text:
You kneel next to Smiurl and put your hand on his shoulder.#

$c"We'll get through this. Together. We always do."$C#

He looks up at you, searching your face for certainty.#
You are not entirely sure you have any,# but you hold his gaze.# Steady.##

After a moment, a faint warmth returns to his skin.#
Not bright.# Not cheerful.# But present.#
A dim, stubborn amber, like the last ember in a dying fire.#

$Y"Together."$C He echoes quietly.#

Corolla watches from above, and you catch the slightest nod.

You crouch beside him and look him in the eyes.#

$c"I won't lie to you, Smiurl. This is worse than anything we've faced. Those people..."$C#

You trail off.# You do not need to finish.#

His lower lip trembles.# For a moment you think he might cry.##
But then something shifts in his expression.#

$Y"Then we need to stop it."$C#

His voice is barely above a whisper, but it is steady.#
And as he says it, a single point of fierce, defiant gold flickers to life on his chest,#
right over his heart.##

$c<<Braver than any of us.>>#

$CCorolla puts a hand on his head. $R"Damn right we do."$C

You press on through the last stretches of the Mazerock in tense silence.#
The canyons gradually widen.# The walls drop lower.# The sky opens above you,#
and the oppressive weight of stone and shadow finally lifts from your shoulders.##

And then you see it.###

$WAyonn.$C###

A city of gold.##

It rises from the desert plain like a mirage,# shimmering towers and domes
glowing with a warm, honeyed light that has no visible source.#
The city seems to pulse gently against the haze of the horizon,#
beautiful,# impossible,## perfect.###

$c<<It's... incredible.>>#

$CBut something is wrong.##

You stare harder.# The light does not shift.# There are no shadows moving across the buildings,#
no flicker of activity on the walls.# The golden shimmer is too uniform, too constant.#
Like a photograph someone is holding up against the sky.##

And as you walk closer,# a feeling grows in the pit of your stomach.#
Not fear, exactly.# Something older.# A deep, primal $Wrepulsion$C,#
as if every cell in your body is telling you to turn around.##

$c<<My skin is crawling.# Every step forward feels like pushing through water.>>#

$CEfeliah has stopped walking.#

$g"It is a projection."$C# She says quietly. $g"A psychic projection. The whole city... what you are seeing... it is not real."$C#

She pauses.#

$g"But what is behind it is."$C

nodes.recap: approach Ayonn or find another way
nodes.text:
The golden illusion of Ayonn shimmers before you, impossibly beautiful, impossibly wrong.#
The psychic repulsion is like a steady pressure against your temples, growing stronger with every step.#
Smiurl is whimpering.# Even Theo's jaw is clenched.#
Corolla walks with her eyes fixed forward, knuckles white on her knife hilts.##

Behind that shimmering veil, a real city waits.# And whoever controls those soldiers.

$c<<Every fibre of my being says go back...# but I won't.>>#

choices.desc: Press forward. You've come too far to stop now.
Maybe there's another way in. Look for a gap in the shield.

nodes.text:
You spend what feels like an hour circling the perimeter.#
The shimmer extends in every direction,# a perfect dome of golden light
with no gaps, no seams, no weakness you can discern.#

Every direction you try, the repulsion intensifies.#
Your head throbs.# Your vision blurs at the edges.#
Smiurl has stopped trying to hide his fear and is openly clinging to Theo's belt.##

Math shakes his head grimly.

$m"It is a complete psychic sphere. There is no gap. No back door.
Whoever built this intended it to be impenetrable."$C

Corolla kicks a rock in frustration.#

$R"Then we go through the front."$C

You approach Ayonn's barrier from the west, skirting along the base of a rocky outcrop
that provides some cover from the city's watchtowers.##

Up close, the energy shield is visible — barely.#
A faint shimmer in the air, like heat haze, but too regular. Too perfect.#
When you reach out your hand, you feel it — a wall of resistance,
like pushing against a strong current of water.# Your fingers tingle, then burn.##

The golden illusion is almost gone now. This close, you can see through it
to the grey reality beneath. The city looks sick.# Tired.# Old.

dialogues.line:
This is it. Efeliah and I will focus our combined psychic energy on a single point.
Kriss... we need you too.
dialogues.comment: He looks at you with an intensity you have never seen from him before.

dialogues.line: Me?# I am not a telepath, Math. I can barely receive thoughts, let alone-

Not telepathy.# Something... else.#
You felt it too, Math. In the desert. At Seer's Rock.
His mind is different.
dialogues.comment: She looks at you, and her expression is soft, but serious.

dialogues.line:
She is right. There is a force in you that is not telepathy.#
The same force that saved Smiurl from the oxengutter, that has been growing since we left Seer's Rock.
We will feed you our strength.# You push through. With everything you have.
dialogues.comment: He puts both hands on your shoulders and looks into your eyes.

dialogues.line: Do not think. Feel.# When the moment comes, you will know what to do.

dialogues.precomment: Corolla steps forward, hand on the hilt of her blade.
dialogues.line:
And while they are busy doing...# whatever it is they are about to do,#
Theo and I will make sure nothing interrupts them.
dialogues.comment: She gives you a look.# That look.# The one that says: $Rdon't you dare die.$C

dialogues.line: Ready.
I will... I will stay close to you, Kriss.
dialogues.comment:
He is still frightened, but he takes a step closer to you.#
A faint glow returns to his skin.# Not much.# But it is there.# And that is everything.

nodes.text:
You walk towards the shimmer.##

Math stands to your left.# Efeliah to your right.#
Their hands close around yours, one on each side, and immediately you feel it:#
a warmth flooding up your arms and into your chest,# like stepping into sunlight after a long winter.##

The golden wall looms before you.# Ten paces.# Five.# Two.##

You reach out with your free hand# and touch the barrier.###

$WPAIN.$C###

A wall of static slams into your skull.# Every nerve in your body ignites at once,#
as if a million needles are being driven through your skin from the inside.#
Your teeth clench so hard you taste blood.#
Your vision whites out.##

$c<<PUSH.>>#

$CYou push.# Not with your hands.# With something behind your eyes,#
something in the base of your skull that has been stirring since the canyon,#
since the oxengutter,# since the sword first hummed in your grip.##

The shield pushes back.# Harder.#
Your nose starts bleeding.# Hot copper on your lips.#
Your knees buckle but you will not fall.##

$m<<Hold on, Kriss. We are here. We are with you.>>#

$CMath's voice.# An anchor in the static.#
You feel his mind beside yours, disciplined and sharp as a lance.#
And Efeliah's strength flowing into you like a river,#
gentle and immense and impossibly deep.##

But it is not enough.##

The shield tightens.# The pain redoubles.#
You feel the controlling intelligence behind it $Wnotice you$C.#
A vast, cold, mechanical awareness turns its attention to the three tiny minds
trying to breach its wall.##

You are a fist trying to punch through a mountain.#
Your grip on Math's hand is slipping.# Your vision is failing.#
Darkness crowds in at the edges.###

You are going to fail.##

$c<<I can't...# I can't do this...>>#

And then something gives way.##

Not from outside.# From within.#
Deep in the base of your skull,# something that has been building since the canyon,#
since the oxengutter,# since the sword first hummed in your grip —#
it erupts.##

$CSomething detonates inside you.##

It is not telepathy.# It is something else entirely.#
A force that erupts from your core like a shockwave,#
channelled through every nerve,# every synapse,#
through Math and Efeliah and the link between you,#
and it hits the shield like a battering ram made of light.###

The world $Wshatters$C.##

A sound like a thousand windows breaking at once.#
A rush of cold air.# The golden shimmer fractures,# spiderwebs,# collapses.#

And then vertigo.# The ground tilts.# The sky spins.##

You fall.##

Someone catches you.##

Blackness.

Fragments.##

You are being carried.# Voices, muffled, as if heard through water.#

You force your eyes open for one searing instant.##

Grey stone.# Not golden.# Narrow streets lined with squat, utilitarian buildings.#
No shimmer.# No light.# Just cold, grey, oppressive stone under a colourless sky.##

$c<<It was all a lie...>>#

$CBlank-faced citizens shuffle past on the street below,# headbands gleaming.#
None of them react to your presence.# None of them react to anything.##

A large building looms ahead, taller than the rest, its facade stained and windowless.#
Something about it pulls at your mind, even now,# like a signal you cannot quite tune in.##

You catch a flash of red.# Corolla, running alongside,# her face taut with worry.#
Theo's arms are around you.# He is carrying you and Math both, one under each arm,#
as if you weigh nothing at all.##

Smiurl is clinging to Theo's back, his whole body pulsing with a terrified, strobing amber.##

Efeliah is walking under her own power, barely,# one hand against the wall to steady herself.##

The metallic taste of blood fills your mouth.#
Your head is splitting.#
The last thing you see before the darkness takes you again
is the tall building growing larger,# closer,#
swallowing the grey sky above you.###

$c<<We made it...>>#

$CBlackness.# Total and absolute.##
